title: Shark Park

infobox.value: Pointsbet Stadium Southern Cross Group Stadium Remondis Stadium Toyota Park Ronson Field Caltex Field Endeavour Field
[Woolooware, New South Wales](/wiki/Woolooware,_New_South_Wales "Woolooware, New South Wales")
22,302 – Sharks vs [St George Illawarra](/wiki/St_George_Illawarra_Dragons "St George Illawarra Dragons"), 2004

or **Endeavour Field**,[[3]](#cite_note-new-name-16-3)[[4]](#cite_note-Austadiums-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) is a [rugby league](/wiki/Rugby_league "Rugby league") stadium in the southern [Sydney](/wiki/Sydney "Sydney") suburb of [Woolooware, New South Wales](/wiki/Woolooware,_New_South_Wales "Woolooware, New South Wales"), Australia. It is the home ground of the Cronulla Sharks Rugby League Club, which represents the [Cronulla](/wiki/Cronulla,_New_South_Wales "Cronulla, New South Wales") and [Sutherland Shire](/wiki/Sutherland_Shire "Sutherland Shire") areas in the [National Rugby League](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League") competition. The Sharks are as of 2023 just one of two professional sporting clubs in Australia (excluding the [Australian Football League](/wiki/Australian_Football_League "Australian Football League")'s ownership of [Docklands Stadium](/wiki/Docklands_Stadium "Docklands Stadium")) that own and operate their home ground (alongside the [Dolphins](/wiki/Dolphins_(NRL) "Dolphins (NRL)") via their parent club [Redcliffe](/wiki/Redcliffe_Dolphins "Redcliffe Dolphins") who compete in the [QRL](/wiki/Queensland_Rugby_League "Queensland Rugby League") with their home ground, [Kayo Stadium](/wiki/Dolphin_Stadium_(Brisbane) "Dolphin Stadium (Brisbane)")) as well as [Western United](/wiki/Western_United_FC "Western United FC")'s future home ground [Wyndham City Stadium](/wiki/Wyndham_City_Stadium "Wyndham City Stadium"). The [Sharkies Leagues Club](/wiki/Sharkies_Leagues_Club "Sharkies Leagues Club") sits beside the stadium.

["Sharks announce Ocean Protect as landmark stadium partner"](https://www.sharks.com.au/news/2025/11/25/sharks-announce-ocean-protect-as-landmark-stadium-partner/). *sharks.com.au*. 25 November 2025. Retrieved 25 November 2025.

Riccio, David (11 March 2016). ["Cronulla Sharks land naming sponsorship for stadium ahead of NRL season opener"](http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/cronulla-sharks-land-naming-sponsorship-for-stadium-ahead-of-nrl-season-opener/news-story/e232e3f65ca9731efecdebacd174ed6b). *The Daily Telegraph*. Retrieved 11 March 2016.

["Sell out confirmed for blockbuster opening home game"](https://www.sharks.com.au/news/2025/03/20/sell-out-confirmed-for-blockbuster-opening-home-game/). Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks. 20 March 2025. Retrieved 25 March 2025.

Taylor, Ashley (24 April 2024). ["This week in history: Sharks unveil Endeavour Stand"](https://www.sharks.com.au/news/2024/04/24/this-week-in-history-sharks-unveil-endeavour-stand/). *Cronulla Sharks*. NRL. Retrieved 15 May 2024.

[Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system"): [34°2′19″S 151°8′27″E﻿ / ﻿34.03861°S 151.14083°E﻿ / -34.03861; 151.14083](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Endeavour_Field&params=34_2_19_S_151_8_27_E_)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Shark Park](/w/index.php?title=Shark_Park&redirect=no "Shark Park"))

Rugby league stadium in Woolooware, New South Wales, Australia

Ocean Protect Stadium

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Map Interactive map of Ocean Protect Stadium | |

| Construction | |

| Tenants | |
| [Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks](/wiki/Cronulla-Sutherland_Sharks "Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks") ([NRL](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League")) (1968–2019, 2022–present)  [Olympic Sharks](/wiki/Olympic_Sharks "Olympic Sharks") ([NSL](/wiki/National_Soccer_League "National Soccer League")) (2001–2003) | |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/85/Shark_Park_front.jpg/250px-Shark_Park_front.jpg)](/wiki/File:Shark_Park_front.jpg)

Endeavour Field from the front.

**Sharks Stadium**,[[1]](#cite_note-Sharks_Stadium-1) known under sponsorship as **Ocean Protect Stadium**,[[2]](#cite_note-2) and historically as **Shark Park** or **Endeavour Field**,[[3]](#cite_note-new-name-16-3)[[4]](#cite_note-Austadiums-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) is a [rugby league](/wiki/Rugby_league "Rugby league") stadium in the southern [Sydney](/wiki/Sydney "Sydney") suburb of [Woolooware, New South Wales](/wiki/Woolooware,_New_South_Wales "Woolooware, New South Wales"), Australia. It is the home ground of the Cronulla Sharks Rugby League Club, which represents the [Cronulla](/wiki/Cronulla,_New_South_Wales "Cronulla, New South Wales") and [Sutherland Shire](/wiki/Sutherland_Shire "Sutherland Shire") areas in the [National Rugby League](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League") competition. The Sharks are as of 2023 just one of two professional sporting clubs in Australia (excluding the [Australian Football League](/wiki/Australian_Football_League "Australian Football League")'s ownership of [Docklands Stadium](/wiki/Docklands_Stadium "Docklands Stadium")) that own and operate their home ground (alongside the [Dolphins](/wiki/Dolphins_(NRL) "Dolphins (NRL)") via their parent club [Redcliffe](/wiki/Redcliffe_Dolphins "Redcliffe Dolphins") who compete in the [QRL](/wiki/Queensland_Rugby_League "Queensland Rugby League") with their home ground, [Kayo Stadium](/wiki/Dolphin_Stadium_(Brisbane) "Dolphin Stadium (Brisbane)")) as well as [Western United](/wiki/Western_United_FC "Western United FC")'s future home ground [Wyndham City Stadium](/wiki/Wyndham_City_Stadium "Wyndham City Stadium"). The [Sharkies Leagues Club](/wiki/Sharkies_Leagues_Club "Sharkies Leagues Club") sits beside the stadium.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Endeavour_Field&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The stadium was built in 1966 and currently has a capacity of 20,000. The first match to be played at the ground came on the 30 June 1968 where Cronulla-Sutherland defeated [Parramatta](/wiki/Parramatta_Eels "Parramatta Eels") 10-7.[[4]](#cite_note-Austadiums-4)

In 1991 the original western grandstand was demolished and the new Endeavour Stand was built in its place for the 1992 season.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

The [Cronulla-Sutherland Rugby League Club](/wiki/Cronulla-Sutherland_Sharks "Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks") owns the stadium as well as the adjacent Leagues Club, one of only two [NRL](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League") clubs to own its own stadium. State government authorities and local councils generally own sporting venues in Australia.

On 21 April 2006 the Federal Government announced a [A$](/wiki/Australian_dollar "Australian dollar")9.6 million grant to the Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks to upgrade the stadium. The upgrade included a new covered stand to seat over 1,500 spectators at the southern end. The new stand was completed in time for the 2008 season.

Renovations were also planned for the ET Stand (originally the Endeavour Stand), named for Cronulla club legend [Andrew Ettingshausen](/wiki/Andrew_Ettingshausen "Andrew Ettingshausen"), and the Peter Burns Stand.

As of June 2020 the Leagues Club was undergoing redevelopment, with the Leagues Club closing its operations on 15 December 2019,[[7]](#cite_note-7) with the facility originally due to reopen in early 2022.[[8]](#cite_note-8) Due to the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic") impacting construction timelines at the Leagues Club site, the completion date has been delayed to early 2023,[[9]](#cite_note-9) but Cronulla returned to playing home games at the stadium in 2022 with a temporary capacity of 12,000 in place until mid-2023.[[10]](#cite_note-10) Further delays in construction resulted in a new completion date of mid-2024 announced in July 2023.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

## Ground usage

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Endeavour_Field&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Ground usage")]

### Rugby league

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Endeavour_Field&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Rugby league")]

In the [NRL](/wiki/NRL "NRL") competition, the [Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks](/wiki/Cronulla-Sutherland_Sharks "Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks") team has been playing at this venue since midway through the [1968 NSWRFL season](/wiki/1968_NSWRFL_season "1968 NSWRFL season"),[[12]](#cite_note-12) which was the club's second season into the competition (they had previously played at [Sutherland Oval](/wiki/Sutherland_Oval "Sutherland Oval") from 1967 until 1968).

High turnout in crowd numbers are usual when Cronulla play local-derby rivals, the [St. George Illawarra Dragons](/wiki/St._George_Illawarra_Dragons "St. George Illawarra Dragons"). The ground attendance record is 22,302, which was set when Cronulla took on St. George in May 2004. The biggest victory Cronulla has recorded at the ground came on the 21 August 2005 against rivals [Manly](/wiki/Manly_Sea_Eagles "Manly Sea Eagles") where Cronulla won 68-6.

In late 2017, the ground hosted the [2017 Women's Rugby League World Cup](/wiki/2017_Women%27s_Rugby_League_World_Cup "2017 Women's Rugby League World Cup") tournament. It played host to the group stages and semi-finals matches.

During the [2019 NRL season](/wiki/2019_NRL_season "2019 NRL season"), it was announced that the ground and the Cronulla leagues club would be undergoing renovations and as a result Cronulla revealed that for the next two years that they would be playing home matches away from their spiritual home with [Kogarah Oval](/wiki/Kogarah_Oval "Kogarah Oval"), [WIN Stadium](/wiki/WIN_Stadium "WIN Stadium") and the new [Western Sydney Stadium](/wiki/Western_Sydney_Stadium "Western Sydney Stadium") as new temporary home grounds.[[13]](#cite_note-13)[[14]](#cite_note-14)

In the early 2020s, Shark Park came under scrutiny by fans and the media due to the fact the venue hosted finals matches despite it's limited capacity and alleged poor facilities. NRL CEO Andrew Abdo stated in September 2025 that the NRL would be reviewing their finals venue policy saying "We haven't changed or reviewed the home finals policy for a while, and I think we're due for that, In this particular case, you have two Sydney teams [on Saturday], and you want as many fans as possible enjoying it live. The players enjoy that, and so do the fans. We need to focus on our fans and the fan experience. There comes a time you need to consider a minimum standard, potentially for finals, that might be above the regular season – the time is right to have a good, close look at it".[[15]](#cite_note-15)

### Football

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Endeavour_Field&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Football")]

The [Sydney Olympic FC](/wiki/Sydney_Olympic_FC "Sydney Olympic FC") club played at the ground for two seasons, from 2001 until 2003, in the former [National Soccer League](/wiki/National_Soccer_League "National Soccer League").

## Naming rights

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Endeavour_Field&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Naming rights")]

The ground has had numerous naming rights deals. Names of this ground over the years have been:

* Endeavour Field
* Ronson Field
* Caltex Field
* Shark Park
* Toyota Park
* Toyota Stadium
* Remondis Stadium
* Southern Cross Group Stadium
* PointsBet Stadium
* Ocean Protect Stadium

  Exterior of main stand
